title: Endicott baseball falls to Denison in hard-fought DIII  National Championship Series
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Endicott College's baseball team was runner-up in the Division III National Championship, losing the decisive Game 3 to Denison 4-3.
The Gulls achieved their first appearance in a National Championship, having won five consecutive NCAA Regional Championships.
Endicott forced a Game 3 with a dramatic extra-inning victory in Game 2, overcoming a four-run deficit to win 11-10.

title: Laxative-laced brownies rattle Nantucket School Committee meeting
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Meghan Perry presented laxative-laced brownies at a Nantucket School Committee meeting, protesting a turf field proposal.
Perry compared the brownies to PFAS contamination, highlighting concerns about artificial turf health impacts.
Nantucket Police are investigating the incident; no charges or consumption of brownies reported.

title: Former McDonald’s manager accused of spitting in ex-girlfriend’s fries arraigned on felony charge
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kaylie Santos, 22, arraigned on a felony charge for allegedly spitting in ex-girlfriend's fries at a Southbridge McDonald's.
Incident was recorded on video, shared on social media, leading to a police investigation in early May.
Southbridge McDonald's owners cooperating with authorities; no other customers affected, according to police.

title: How many players of Giannis Antetokounmpo’s caliber have been traded?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Giannis Antetokounmpo has seven All-NBA first-team selections, tied for 6th all-time with Wilt Chamberlain.
Only five players with comparable All-NBA accolades, like LeBron James and Kareem Abdul-Jabbar, have ever been traded.
The Boston Celtics are considered a 'wild card' in potential trade discussions involving Antetokounmpo.

title: Review & setlist: In Boston, St. Vincent goes symphonic to stunning effect
author: Mary Nordmann
bullets: St. Vincent performed with the Boston Pops at Symphony Hall on June 4, 2026, enhancing her music with symphonic arrangements.
Annie Clark collaborated with conductor Jules Buckley to integrate a 60-piece orchestra into her live performances across the U.S.
The setlist included songs like "Digital Witness," "Los Ageless," and an encore featuring "Candy Darling" and "Slow Disco."

title: 5 storylines to follow as the Red Sox and Yankees battle in the Bronx
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: The Red Sox face the Yankees in a three-game series following their April sweep by New York.
Sonny Gray and Cam Schlittler headline pitching matchups, with Suárez and Early looking to improve their performances.
Yankees' Ben Rice and Red Sox's Willson Contreras and their recent form are key to their team's successes.

title: Canton, Norfolk DA issue statements on texts included in Karen Read’s new lawsuit
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Former Canton Sgt. Sean Goode resigned amid an internal probe into offensive text messages exposed in Karen Read's lawsuit.
Ex-Trooper Michael Proctor and Goode exchanged derogatory messages implicating misconduct during the John O’Keefe case investigation.
Norfolk DA's Office is reviewing cases involving Proctor to ensure fair prosecution after offensive messages were revealed.

title: ‘Old Thom,’ iconic solitary orca, spotted by Cape Cod fishermen
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: 'Old Thom,' a solitary orca with a unique dorsal fin, was spotted again off Cape Cod by fishermen.
Old Thom has been seen around Cape Cod multiple times since 2015, often swimming alone or with smaller dolphins.
Orcas are social animals, but 'Old Thom' is known for its solitary nature, contrasting typical orca group behavior.

title: Mass. House approves later bar hours, outdoor drinking zones for World Cup
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Massachusetts House approved a pilot program allowing extended bar hours until 3 a.m. from June 8 through July 31.
The bill proposes designated outdoor drinking districts to coincide with major events like FIFA World Cup matches and the Tall Ships.
The measure aims to boost business during summer events, but some workers express concerns over late-night shifts and transportation.

title: ‘It’s not done yet,’ Karen Read warns on ‘Today’ show after filing explosive new lawsuit
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Karen Read appeared on the 'Today' show following a lawsuit against police, alleging corruption in her murder charge case.
Read, acquitted in 2025 of killing Boston Officer John O'Keefe, claims ex-officers sent biased messages indicating police misconduct.
Ex-Trooper Michael Proctor was fired and Sgt. Sean Goode resigned amid investigations linked to Read's lawsuit and misconduct claims.
